heading: Salvaging operations by foreign vessels
label: 80104 Salvaging operations by foreign vessels
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)80104(a)46 App.:316(d) (words before proviso).R.S. § 4370(d), (e); , .June 11, 1940, ch. 32454 Stat. 30580104(b)46 App.:316(d) (proviso).80104(c)46 App.:316(e).In subsection (a), the words “authorized by a treaty” are omitted because they are restated in subsection (c).In subsection (b), before paragraph (1), the Secretary of Homeland Security is substituted for the Commissioner of Customs because the functions of the Customs Service and of the Secretary of the Treasury relating thereto were transferred to the Secretary of Homeland Security by section 403(1) of the Homeland Security Act of 2002 (, ). The functions of the Commissioner of Customs previously were vested in the Secretary of the Treasury under . For prior related transfers of functions, see the transfer of functions note under 46 App. U.S.C. 316. The words “and no penalty shall be incurred for such authorized use” are omitted as unnecessary.Pub. L. 107–296116 Stat. 2178section 321(c) of title 31In subsection (b)(1), the words “(including a Bowaters corporation under )” are added because the source for section 12118 (46 App. U.S.C. 883–1) provides that a corporation meeting the requirements of that section is also a citizen of the United States under 46 App. U.S.C. 316, restated in part in this section.section 12118 of this titleSubsection (b)(2) is substituted for “documented under the laws of the United States or numbered pursuant to the Act of , as amended ()” to refer to the current provisions on documentation and numbering.June 7, 191846 U.S.C. 288In subsection (c), the words “authorized by treaty, including” are added because the words “authorized by a treaty” are omitted from subsection (a).

children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: a .—Prohibition
children.text: Except as provided in this section or , a foreign vessel may not, under penalty of forfeiture, engage in salvaging operations on the Atlantic or Pacific coast of the United States, in any portion of the Great Lakes or their connecting or tributary waters, including any portion of the Saint Lawrence River through which the international boundary line extends, or in territorial waters of the United States on the Gulf of Mexico.section 80105 of this title
owned only by citizens of the United States (including a Bowaters corporation under ); andsection 12118 of this title
documented under chapter 121 of this title or numbered under chapter 123 of this title.

article II of the Treaty between the United States and Great Britain concerning reciprocal rights for United States and Canada in the conveyance of prisoners and wrecking and salvage, signed at Washington, (); orMay 18, 190835 Stat. 2036
the Treaty between the United States of America and Mexico to facilitate assistance to and salvage of vessels in territorial waters, signed at Mexico City, ().June 13, 193549 Stat. 3359
